hawkish_phrases: considerable uncertainty remains around the labour market after the government job support schemes unwind
there remains a risk of a more persistent period of elevated unemployment
recent increases in Covid-19 cases...have the potential to weigh further on economic activity
dovish_phrases: The Committee does not intend to tighten monetary policy until there is clear evidence that significant progress is being made in eliminating spare capacity and achieving the 2% inflation target sustainably
stands ready to adjust monetary policy accordingly
The unemployment rate was projected to rise markedly, consistent with a material degree of spare capacity
CPI inflation is expected to remain below 1% until early 2021
given the risks, it is unclear how informative they are about how the economy will perform further out
summary: The MPC unanimously maintained Bank Rate at 0.1% and continued asset purchases at £745 billion, with explicit forward guidance that it does not intend to tighten until significant progress is made in eliminating spare capacity and achieving the 2% inflation target sustainably, amid projected elevated unemployment and below-target inflation.
